# PrimaSTEM — Full Site Content

> Screen-free coding and mathematics for children ages 4–10. Wooden programmable robot with tangible NFC command tokens. Made in Cannes, France.

---

# Home (/)

Coding and math without a screen.

The only screen-free tool that teaches both. Ages 4 to 10. Made from wood.

From €210/kit at 100+ units/year.

## Two levels — one device

One purchase — lasts through primary school.

Logic, Numbers — Ages 4–7: Logical step 15 cm (adjustable), 90° turns. Mazes, sequences, debugging.

Mathematics, Coding — Ages 7–10: Precise distances and angles of any value. Arithmetic, geometry, marker drawing.

## The robot draws what the child programs

The robot drew them following a child's program. Pentagon, Star, Spiral, Heart.

Example: (Repeat 5) [(Forward) (50), (Right) (144)] = a star.

## Works with maps you already have

PrimaSTEM adapts to the content of other educational robots. Step size is adjustable — 10, 12, 15, 25 cm. Any grid map works. No new materials needed.

## See it in action

Watch how children learn coding and math with PrimaSTEM. Video recorded with a prototype. Final product may differ.

## Choose your path

Complete kit: robot, control board, command tokens, lesson plans and teacher's guide.

School: available through our distributors. Robot + control board + tokens. Lesson plans & teacher's guide online.

Distributor: from €210/kit at 100+ units/year. €235 standard, €200 at 500+. You set your own retail. Marketing support. Training included.

Parent: available through our distributors. Complete kit, ready to use. No subscriptions, no apps. No hidden costs. Lasts through primary school.

## Trusted by our partners

Already working in 5 countries. Partners: Ministry of Education Luxembourg, La Compagnie du Code, Terra Numerica, A4 Éducation, AFINEF.

---

# Product (/product)

## How PrimaSTEM works

1. Place tokens left to right. Green LED = accepted. The board speaks the command.
2. Press "Start." The robot executes. LEDs show which command is running.
3. Check the result. Wrong? Find the error, swap a token, run again.

## What's in the kit

Ladybug robot: 125 mm diameter, wood, USB-C charging, 2–3 hours battery.
Control board: 317 × 217 mm, wood, 11 dual slots, Bluetooth ~5 m.
Command tokens: 33 × 33 mm, cardboard, NFC 13.56 MHz, Scratch color coding, writable via phone app.
Teacher's guide: Lesson plans online, activities & printables, docs.primastem.com, 10 languages.

## Commands

Movement & Program: Forward, Backward, Left, Right, Random, Function, Pause.
Repeat: 2–999, Random repeat 1–6.
Mathematics: +, −, ×, ÷, √, ^
Numbers: 1–999
System: Default step size, calibration.

## Drawing

Insert a marker. Every movement leaves a line. Forward 50, Right 144, Repeat 5 = a star. Add an arithmetic progression — the star turns into a spiral. These shapes are programmed by children, drawn by the robot.

## Works with any map

Step size is adjustable. 10, 12, 15, 25 cm — any grid. Use maps from any robot already in your classroom. No new materials needed.

## Open platform

Every token is a standard NFC tag (13.56 MHz). Write any code using a free phone app. Create your own numbers, loops or arithmetic operations. NFC tags on the playing field — the robot plays audio when it drives over them. Language cards, musical fields, story quests.

## Voice feedback

The board speaks each token as it's placed in 14 languages: English, Français, Русский, Українська, Deutsch, Español, Italiano, Nederlands, Norsk, Polski, Svenska, 日本語, Português (Brasil), Türkçe. Helps children who can't read yet and children with visual impairments. Braille version in pilot testing.

## Specifications

Material: Wood. Robot: 125 mm diameter, 44 mm H. Control board: 317 × 217 mm. Tokens: 33 × 33 mm, cardboard, NFC 13.56 MHz. Connectivity: Bluetooth ~5 m. Battery: Li-Ion USB-C, 2–3 hours. Default step: 15 cm (any value, adjustable). Angles: 1°–360°. Mathematics: +, −, ×, ÷, √, ^, !. Slots: 6×2 main + 5×2 function. Audio: Voice feedback in 14 languages + sound samples. Certification: CE. Age: 4–10. Made in: Cannes, France.

From €210/kit at 100+ units/year. Complete kit, ready to use.

---

# Distributors (/distributors)

A screen-free education tool the market is asking for. Schools, preschools, Montessori, after-school programs, parents — one product for all. Coding + mathematics. Ages 4–10. Wood. CE + UKCA certified. 17 languages. Wholesale from €210/kit at 100+ units/year.

## The market

PISA 2022: math scores are declining across Europe. Governments are restricting screens in schools. Schools need screen-free STEM tools — but the ones available only teach basic directions. PrimaSTEM combines tangible programming with real mathematics. Arbitrary angles, arithmetic operations, geometric drawing. One product for ages 4–10. A school buys once and uses it for years.

## What sells

Two age groups — one device. Works with existing maps. The robot draws. Teachers create their own tokens. Braille version in pilot testing.

## Terms

Wholesale: from €210/kit at 100+ units/year — €235 standard, €200 at 500+, excl. VAT. You set your own retail and margin. Marketing materials in your language. Technical support. Joint participation at trade shows. Territory exclusivity negotiable. Early pilots and partners: Luxembourg (Ministry of Education pilot), Belgium (Code N Play), France. All terms, pricing and exclusivity conditions are discussed individually.

---

# Schools (/schools)

Coding and mathematics, ages 4–10. One device. No screen, no apps. Works with maps from any robots you already have. Available through our distributors.

## What children do

They place command tokens on the board — that's their program, visible in full. Press "Start." The robot executes. Wrong result? Find the error, swap a token, run again. Sequences, functions, loops, debugging. No screen. Hands only.

## Two levels

Ages 4–7: Logic. Logical steps, 90° turns. Forward, backward, left, right, function, repeat. Planning, sequences, debugging, counting.
Ages 7–10: Mathematics. Precise distances in mm and any angle in degrees: 1°–360°. Arithmetic: +, −, ×, ÷, √, ^. Negative numbers. Progressions. Insert a marker — the robot draws polygons, stars, spirals, fractals.

## Lessons included

Objectives, materials, step-by-step instructions, printables. Full documentation at docs.primastem.com — 10 languages.

## What teachers don't expect

Open platform. Sound maps. Voice feedback. Braille version in pilot testing.

Available through our distributors. Volume pricing on request.

---

# Parents (/parents)

Real skills, real play, no screen. A wooden robot your child programs by hand. Ages 4–10. Available through our distributors.

## What is it

Tokens on a wooden board — "forward," "turn right," "repeat 3 times." That's the program. The child sees it, touches it, rearranges it. Presses a button — the robot executes.

## Grows with your child

At 4–5: Simple commands and numbers. Mazes on colorful maps.
At 6–7: Functions and loops.
At 8–10: Mathematics. Precise angles, distances, arithmetic. The robot draws stars, spirals, geometric patterns.

## Why this one

Wood. Not plastic. No screen. No apps. It speaks. It draws. Made in France. CE certified.

Available through our distributors. Complete kit.

---

# About (/about)

French startup from Cannes building screen-free educational technology.

## We make tools that help children think

PrimaSTEM is inspired by Seymour Papert's constructionism and Montessori principles.

## How it started

We watched children spending more time in front of screens. We watched math scores fall. And we watched a child's eyes light up when they touched a wooden board and a robot, pressed a button — and the robot did exactly what they told it to.

## What we believe

Children learn by doing. Mathematics is beautiful. Technology should be open. Simplicity takes work.

## Where we are

Partners: Terra Numerica / INRIA / CNRS, Educazur, AFINEF. Early traction: Luxembourg (Ministry of Education pilot), Belgium (Code N Play), France. Trade shows: EDUCATECH (Paris), SIMO Educación (Madrid), SpielwarenMesse (Nuremberg).

---

We respond within 24 hours. Working languages: English, Français, Русский.

# FAQ (/faq)

Q: What age is PrimaSTEM for?
A: From age 4. Ages 4–7 work with logic and simple programs. Ages 7–10 add mathematics, precise angles, and drawing.

Q: How does it work?
A: Children place command tokens on a wooden board — that's their program. Press Start — the robot executes. No screen, no apps.

Q: What's in the kit?
A: Ladybug robot (wood, USB-C, Bluetooth), control board with 11 dual slots, command tokens (cardboard, NFC, color-coded), and a teacher's guide with lesson plans.

Q: Does the robot draw?
A: Yes. Insert a marker and every movement leaves a line. Children program stars, spirals, polygons, fractals.

Q: Does it work with maps from other robots?
A: Yes. The default step size is adjustable — 10, 12, 15, 25 cm or any value.

Q: How much does it cost?
A: Distributors: from €210/kit at 100+ units/year — €235 standard, €200 at 500+. Schools and families: available through our distributors — contact us for a partner in your country.

Q: Can I rent instead of buying?
A: Rental is available for schools.

Q: Is there a distributor in my country?
A: We're just starting — early pilots and partners in France, Belgium and Luxembourg, with distributor conversations underway across Europe.

Q: How long does the battery last?
A: USB-C charging, 2–3 hours on a single charge.

Q: Is it safe for children?
A: CE certified. Made from wood. No screen. Recommended for ages 4+.

Q: Can teachers create their own tokens?
A: Yes. Every token is a standard NFC tag. Write any code using a free phone app.

Q: Can my child use it without a teacher?
A: Yes. Lessons available on docs.primastem.com in 10 languages. Video tutorials on YouTube.
